message:
fix: make env file discovery more resilient with fallback paths

The run_server_with_token.sh script previously hardcoded a single path
for the config.env file. This change makes it configurable via the
AM_RUST_ENV_FILE environment variable and adds automatic fallback
discovery: it first checks the original config.env location, then
falls back to .env in the same directory. This aligns the shell
wrapper with the Rust binary's own multi-path env file resolution
and prevents startup failures when the config file lives at a
non-default path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2,7 +2,14 @@
 set -euo pipefail
 
 AM_RUST_BIN="/Users/jemanuel/.local/bin/am"
-AM_RUST_ENV_FILE="${HOME}/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env"
+AM_RUST_ENV_FILE_DEFAULT="/Users/jemanuel/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env"
+AM_RUST_ENV_FILE="${AM_RUST_ENV_FILE:-$AM_RUST_ENV_FILE_DEFAULT}"
+if [ ! -f "$AM_RUST_ENV_FILE" ] && [ -f "${HOME}/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env" ]; then
+  AM_RUST_ENV_FILE="${HOME}/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env"
+fi
+if [ ! -f "$AM_RUST_ENV_FILE" ] && [ -f "${HOME}/.config/mcp-agent-mail/.env" ]; then
+  AM_RUST_ENV_FILE="${HOME}/.config/mcp-agent-mail/.env"
+fi
 
 trim_ascii_whitespace() {
   local value="${1:-}"
